event: View of Peter the Great's Cabin and Canal in Leningrad
symbols: the small yellow cabin — Peter the Great's modest first dwelling in Saint Petersburg, a historic relic amid the imperial city
the bare trees — winter or early spring, the skeletal branches silhouetted against a pale sky
the canal and stone bridge — the waterways that define the character of Saint Petersburg
the still water with reflections — a mirror of the built environment, doubling the urban geometry
description: A small yellow building with a red roof sits on the bank of a canal in Leningrad (Saint Petersburg). Bare-branched trees rise behind an ornate iron railing fence. A stone arch bridge crosses the canal at right. A small boat is moored in the still water, which reflects the buildings, trees, and bridge. The palette is muted -- browns, ochres, grays, and pale sky -- rendered in a loose, impressionistic watercolor style with visible brushwork. The scene is quiet, wintry, and empty of human figures.

_meta.title: Leningrad. Cabin of Peter the Great
